conditional_claims.summary: Software-account private-key use is modeled as vault-mediated and authentication-gated, but native vault cryptographic implementation is outside reviewed TypeScript source.
Signing requires account selection, review acceptance, authentication/vault overlay, and signed-object callback values before a signed blob is accepted.
Remote payload fetch verifies request JSON digest and non-generated approvals revalidate payload state before signing.
Client-side replay controls block resolved, expired, and locally already-submitted states, but backend atomic single-use behavior is not proved.
Payload preflight and signing enforce forced network, transaction-template network, current network definitions, and non-legacy NetworkID population.
Payment validation models amount, native balance, issued-currency trustline, freeze, and issuer obligation-limit checks.
TrustSet, OfferCreate, and SignerListSet validations are TODO pass-throughs, so safety claims depending on those transaction classes must remain blocked.
Backend payload creation authorization, PATCH conflict handling, expiration, and atomic single-use are required assumptions, not proved facts.
The runtime-trace report has monitor facts but no real-device trace bundle, so source review cannot be treated as deployed runtime equivalence.
The proof consumer must reject disproved, unknown, not-modeled, stale, missing-solver, and blocked-assumption outcomes; this policy is defined here but still requires kernel proof tasks.

required_evidence_to_clear: native vault source or binary audit
external_assumptions.summary: Native vault, keychain, biometric, and passcode cryptographic implementation properties are outside the reviewed TypeScript model.
Backend payload authorization, single-use, expiration, and PATCH conflict behavior are required but not proved.
XRPL consensus, node honesty, mempool, queue, and finality behavior are outside the client source model.
No real-device runtime trace bundle proves that deployed app behavior matches reviewed source behavior.

required_evidence_to_clear: negative tests for invalid transaction intents
external_assumptions.summary: Selected transaction classes have TODO validation functions and cannot support broad transaction-safety claims.

required_evidence_to_clear: Lean or Coq proof-consumer kernel report
external_assumptions.summary: The proof-consumer kernel still needs to prove receipt binding and rejection of non-proved outcomes.

fail_closed_policy.acceptance_rule: Public source evidence may support facts and conditional claims only; it must never approve a production release without cleared assumptions, vendor evidence, runtime traces, and required solver coverage.

counterexample.violated_policy: blocking assumptions cannot be cleared without evidence

latest_version_tag.name: v3.3.0
latest_version_tag.selection_rule: highest semver tag observed in git ls-remote output

upstream_drift.method: git ls-remote https://github.com/XRPL-Labs/Xaman-App HEAD refs/heads/master refs/heads/main refs/tags/*

solver_coverage_gaps.coverage_gap: SMT agreement only detects blocked assumptions here; it is not a proof of production safety.
Apalache checked the bounded TLA invariants; this remains bounded model evidence, not a proof of deployed runtime or production safety.
Tamarin and ProVerif protocol checks have not run.
Lean policy fixture is checked, but Coq is unavailable and production integration remains unproved.

source_supported_facts.summary: Full-access software accounts require both a private key and an encryption key, then store the private key in the vault under the account public key before the account record is created.
Readonly accounts and Tangem accounts are created without the repository storing a software private key in the vault during account add.

The Realm account model stores address, label, public key, access level, encryption level, optional Tangem metadata, and network-scoped account details such as balance, sequence, regular key, flags, and trust lines.
Realm storage opens with an encryption key obtained from the native vault; an existing Realm file without a stored encryption key is rejected.
The TypeScript vault facade delegates create, open, existence, rekey, purge, storage-key, and clear-storage operations to React Native's VaultManagerModule.
Passcode authentication checks a wrong-attempt backoff, hashes the entered passcode, compares it to stored settings, unlocks on success, and records failed attempts on failure.

When purge-on-bruteforce is enabled, failed passcode attempts warn at the tenth failed attempt and reset the app after more than ten failed attempts by clearing vault storage and deleting the Realm file.
The authentication service shows a lock overlay and marks the app locked when the app has never been unlocked, elapsed realtime moves backward, or the configured auto-lock duration has elapsed.
The generic authentication overlay presents a six-digit passcode input and exposes biometric authorization only when biometrics are available and the caller allows biometric authorization.
Biometric authentication is available only when the stored biometric setting is not None and the device sensor check succeeds; authentication is rejected if requested while the app is not active.

For passcode-protected signing, entering a valid passcode passes the computed passcode hash as the vault encryption key; successful biometrics passes the stored passcode hash as the vault encryption key.
For passphrase-protected signing, the sign button is disabled until a passphrase exists, and signing passes that passphrase as the vault encryption key.

The passphrase account-auth overlay verifies the passphrase by opening the vault using account.publicKey and only calls success after Vault.open returns a private key; the local privateKey variable is cleared before success.

Repository signability includes full-access accounts unless master-key and regular-key conditions block them, and includes readonly accounts only when their regular key is imported as a full-access account.

The Vault overlay rejects readonly accounts without usable regular-key signing, selects a preferred signer, prompts signer selection when both master and regular-key signers are usable, and routes authentication by the preferred signer's encryption level.
Software signing requires a supplied encryption key, a full-access preferred signer with public key, and a successful Vault.open before deriving a private-key signer and signing the transaction.

Physical-encryption Tangem signing obtains card metadata from the account, prepares a raw signing hash, starts a Tangem SDK session, asks the card to sign, and completes the signed object without opening the software vault.

Before showing the Vault overlay, transaction signing rejects missing accounts, duplicate signing, unsupported transaction types for the current network, missing fees, missing account sequence, and aborted transactions.
The signing callback rejects signed results without signed transaction data, signer public key, sign method, or a transaction id for non-pseudo transactions before storing the signed blob.
Ledger submission is rejected unless a signed blob exists, the transaction is not already submitted or submitting, and the transaction is not aborted.
Downgrading a full-access account to readonly purges its vault entry when present, and purging a full-access account purges its vault entry before deleting the account record.

Xaman e2e feature files exercise generated and imported accounts with passcode and passphrase security, signing with passcode and passphrase, passphrase change, and readonly-to-full-access upgrade and downgrade flows.

evidence.notes: Payload.build constructs generated sign requests from local transaction JSON.

source_supported_facts.summary: In-app payload creation builds a local generated payload with a new UUID, submit/pathfinding metadata, signer constraints from TxJson.Account, request JSON, tx_type, and timestamps.

evidence.notes: Lifecycle-relevant payload fields are declared in TypeScript interfaces.

source_supported_facts.summary: Payload metadata and reference types explicitly carry uuid, submit, resolved/signed/cancelled/expired flags, origin return URLs, forced signer list, force_network, created_at, expires_at, request_json, and optional hash.

evidence.notes: Payload.verify compares request_json digest with payload hash.
Payload.fetch sends the digest header and rejects unverified payloads.
Digest serialization sorts object keys and hashes serialized request JSON.

source_supported_facts.summary: Remote payload fetch validates the response payload hash against the canonical request_json digest before accepting it into the review flow.

evidence.notes: Fetch rejects already-resolved and expired payloads.

source_supported_facts.summary: Payload.fetch rejects backend responses that already have response.resolved_at or meta.expired, blocking already-resolved or expired sign requests before review.

evidence.notes: handlePayloadReference validates UUIDs, fetches with PayloadOrigin.QR, and routes to review.
The scan dispatcher routes payload references and transaction templates to dedicated handlers.

source_supported_facts.summary: QR scanning detects Xumm/Xaman payload references, validates UUID shape, fetches the payload with QR origin, and opens the review transaction modal.

evidence.notes: LinkingService adds runtime URL listeners and checks initial URLs.
Deep-link payload references fetch with PayloadOrigin.DEEP_LINK and route to review.
LinkingService dispatches detected payload references into handlePayloadReference.

source_supported_facts.summary: Deep-link handling registers URL listeners, checks the initial URL, detects Xumm/Xaman payload references, validates UUID shape, fetches with DEEP_LINK origin, and opens review.

evidence.notes: Foreground sign-request notifications emit a request-list update.
Notification opens fetch payloads with PUSH_NOTIFICATION origin and route to review.

source_supported_facts.summary: Sign-request push notifications validate the payload UUID, fetch the payload with PUSH_NOTIFICATION origin, and route to the review transaction modal.

evidence.notes: Pending payload API responses are converted to Payload objects.
Events screen subscribes to sign-request and app foreground refresh events.
Events screen loads pending payloads via BackendService.
Request list rows set EVENT_LIST origin and open ReviewTransaction.

source_supported_facts.summary: The events screen loads pending payloads from the backend, refreshes when signRequestUpdate or app foreground events occur, and request rows set EVENT_LIST origin before opening review.

evidence.notes: Payload accessors expose forced signers and forced network.
Preflight runs forced network, transaction creation, and signing-account loading in order.
Forced network mismatch produces a SWITCH_NETWORK preflight error.
Preflight loads signable accounts and applies forced signer filtering.

source_supported_facts.summary: Review preflight checks payload.force_network against the active network and filters available signable accounts against payload signers before the review screen can pass preflight.

evidence.notes: ReviewStep renders transaction-specific and fallback templates.
ReviewStep renders app info, account picker, transaction details, and accept SwipeButton.

source_supported_facts.summary: The review UI displays the request header, application information, signer label, account picker, sign-for-account information, transaction details, developer JSON when enabled, and a slide-to-accept control disabled while the request is not ready.

evidence.notes: Payload.validate re-fetches the payload by UUID.
onAccept validates non-generated payloads before continuing.
Account activation and transaction-specific validation can stop approval before signing.

source_supported_facts.summary: On user acceptance, non-generated payloads are fetched again through Payload.validate before account activation checks, transaction validations, warning overlays, and signing can proceed.

evidence.notes: prepareAndSignTransaction calls transaction.sign and then submit.
Sign.mixin checks signing preconditions and opens the vault overlay.
Sign result must include signed transaction, signer public key, sign method, and transaction id for non-pseudo transactions.

source_supported_facts.summary: After approval validations pass, the modal signs through transaction.sign, which checks supported transaction definitions, prepares fee/sequence fields, rejects already-signed or aborted transactions, and opens the vault overlay for authenticated signing.

evidence.notes: Payload.reject patches backend rejection for non-generated payloads.
Review close and decline paths determine whether to reject, close, or show a reject dialog.

source_supported_facts.summary: Review dismissal rejects non-generated payloads through a backend PATCH carrying reject=true, USER or APP initiator, optional reason, and origin type; XApp and pseudo payload closes directly decline rather than just closing.

evidence.notes: QR transaction templates check NetworkID before building a local payload.
Deep-link transaction templates check NetworkID before building a local payload.
Sign.mixin populates NetworkID on non-legacy networks.
Sign.mixin checks current network transaction definitions before signing.

source_supported_facts.summary: QR/deep-link transaction templates reject network mismatch, preflight enforces force_network, Sign.mixin populates NetworkID for non-legacy networks, and signing rejects transaction types not supported by the current network definitions.

evidence.notes: Payload.patch sends PATCH updates for non-generated payloads.
ReviewTransactionModal creates the signed payload patch and sends it before submission.

source_supported_facts.summary: After signing, the review modal patches the backend with signed_blob, tx_id, signmethod, signpubkey, optional multisign account, and current node/network environment before any optional ledger submission.

evidence.notes: Payload.shouldSubmit excludes pseudo and multisign payloads.
Ledger submit and verification run only when payload.shouldSubmit() is true.
ResultStep has a signed-only result view separate from submitted results.

source_supported_facts.summary: Ledger broadcast is conditional: payload.shouldSubmit requires meta.submit, non-pseudo transaction, and not multisign. Pseudo and multisign requests can be signed and patched without client broadcast.

evidence.notes: Sign.mixin submit guards signed state, duplicate submits, aborted state, and records submit results.

source_supported_facts.summary: Ledger submission requires a signed blob, rejects already-submitted/in-progress transactions, rejects aborted transactions, sets an isSubmitted flag before calling the ledger, and records the submit result.

evidence.notes: ReviewTransactionModal submits, verifies, patches dispatch result, emits update, and moves to result.
SubmittingStep displays the connected network while submitting/verifying.

source_supported_facts.summary: When ledger submission runs, the review modal patches backend dispatch status with target node, node/network type, and engine result, then moves to the result step.

evidence.notes: SubmitRequest models the ledger broadcast boundary.
SubmitResponse records preliminary result and broadcast/queued/kept state.

source_supported_facts.summary: The typed ledger submit request boundary is XRPL command submit with a tx_blob and optional fail_hard; its response records preliminary engine result, acceptance, applied/broadcast/queued/kept booleans, and validated ledger index.
The common transaction base declares TransactionType, Account, Sequence, and LastLedgerSequence as required fields, while Fee, Memos, Signers, and NetworkID are optional common XRPL fields.

normalized_fact.dispatch_source: TransactionTypes enum and genuine transaction exports

source_supported_facts.summary: TransactionFactory reads TransactionType, rejects missing values, dispatches to a matching genuine transaction class when present, and otherwise uses FallbackTransaction.
ValidationFactory selects a transaction-specific validation function by constructing the `${Type}Validation` export name and fails if no validation function is present.

Signing requires an account, rejects already signed transactions and unsupported network transaction types, requires Fee before prepare, sets Sequence when missing, populates LastLedgerSequence, and writes NetworkID only for non-legacy networks.

The Payment class requires Amount and Destination, supports SendMax, DeliverMin, Paths, DestinationTag, and CredentialIDs, and derives delivered amount from metadata with native drops converted to the network native asset.

Payment validation rejects missing or zero Amount, checks native spend against fresh account available balance, checks issued-currency destination trustlines, issuer freezes, sender IOU balances, and issuer obligation limits.
TrustSet requires LimitAmount and exposes currency, issuer, and numeric limit from LimitAmount; its validation function is present but resolves without checks.

OfferCreate requires TakerPays and TakerGets, optionally models Expiration and OfferID, computes rate from taker amounts, and infers offer status from metadata; validation currently resolves without checks.
SignerListSet models SignerQuorum and SignerEntries with the SignerEntries codec; validation currently resolves without checks.
MemoParser treats long hex-like memo data as application/x-binary references and otherwise hex-encodes text/plain Description memos; decode returns binary data without UTF-8 conversion.

AmountParser normalizes numeric strings through BigNumber, rejects fractional drops, converts drops to native by dividing by 1,000,000, and converts native to drops by multiplying by 1,000,000 and rounding to whole drops.
MutationsMixin parses balance changes from metadata, removes separate native fee decreases when multiple debits exist, and deducts the fee from the owner's native balance change for display.

The submit boundary requires a signed tx_blob and returns preliminary XRPL node state including accepted, applied, broadcast, kept, queued, sequence, and ledger-index fields; SignMixin prevents local duplicate submission.

required_evidence_to_model: android native VaultManagerModule implementation
ios native VaultManagerModule implementation
keychain/keystore accessibility and hardware-backed settings
cipher and migration tests
unmodeled_components.summary: The TypeScript facade proves that VaultManagerModule is called, but this artifact does not model the Android or iOS native implementation, keychain accessibility class, cipher mode, hardware-backed storage, migration behavior, or memory-zeroization guarantees.

required_evidence_to_model: native biometric module implementation
platform biometric policy configuration
device enrollment-change tests

required_evidence_to_model: CoreRepository.hashPasscode implementation
core settings schema and persistence protection
KDF parameter review
unmodeled_components.summary: The reviewed authentication service calls CoreRepository.hashPasscode and compares the result, but this artifact does not model the hash or KDF algorithm, salt handling, parameter strength, or stored-passcode protection.

required_evidence_to_model: xrpl-accountlib source and version pin review
Tangem SDK source/version and firmware attestation model
XRPL transaction signing test vectors
unmodeled_components.summary: This artifact models when Xaman calls xrpl-accountlib and Tangem SDK signing paths, but not the correctness of transaction canonicalization, signature generation, raw signing completion, Tangem firmware, or SDK attestation.

required_evidence_to_model: extended Xaman source coverage extractor roots for src/screens/Overlay/Vault
parsed coverage for VaultOverlay and Vault Methods files

This wallet-auth artifact reviewed the manifest-pinned files directly, but extractor completeness for those files remains a separate gap.

required_evidence_to_model: reproducible mobile build evidence
native runtime traces for vault and signing flows
deployed binary provenance
unmodeled_components.summary: The artifact binds source facts at a commit, but does not prove that an app-store binary, build environment, backend dependency, native runtime trace, or device configuration matches these source facts.

evidence.notes: Client fetch checks backend fields but backend enforcement is outside this source file.

required_evidence_to_model: Xaman backend payload API source or formal API contract
server-side tests for payload UUID authorization, resolved_at atomicity, expiration enforcement, and duplicate PATCH rejection
runtime traces showing backend response behavior under replay races
unmodeled_components.summary: The client source shows fetch, validate, patch, reject, and expired/resolved checks, but does not prove backend authorization, payload creation policy, atomic single-use resolution, replay resistance across devices, or PATCH conflict handling.

evidence.notes: ScanModal dispatches decoded types but decoder/native correctness is external.
LinkingService receives OS URLs but OS delivery semantics are not modeled.

required_evidence_to_model: Audited xumm-string-decode implementation and test vectors
Native camera and OS linking behavior evidence
End-to-end QR/deep-link runtime traces for malformed, spoofed, and boundary inputs

evidence.notes: Client verifies by hash through LedgerService, but consensus behavior is not proven here.
SubmitResponse fields are modeled as preliminary ledger/node response state.

required_evidence_to_model: LedgerService implementation and node trust policy review
XRPL consensus assumptions and validator/network model
Runtime traces for submit, queued, failed, and verified ledger outcomes

evidence.notes: The source manifest pins source content but not deployed runtime equivalence.

required_evidence_to_model: Reproducible build attestation for the reviewed commit
Runtime telemetry or e2e traces for QR/deep-link/review/approval/rejection/expiration/replay/broadcast paths
Production backend and node endpoint configuration evidence

required_evidence_to_model: Implemented validation functions for TrustSet, OfferCreate, and SignerListSet
Tests showing rejection of malformed limits, offer amounts, signer quorum, and signer entries
Proof obligations that classify unresolved TODO validation as blocking

required_evidence_to_model: Inventory of every transaction export reachable from TransactionFactory
Per-class source facts and validation semantics for all supported amendments
Solver obligations generated for every high-risk transaction class
unmodeled_components.summary: The artifact reviews representative transaction classes and dispatch mechanics, but it does not model every transaction class, pseudo transaction, parser field, or amendment-specific XRPL rule.

required_evidence_to_model: Reviewed LedgerService and NetworkService source facts
Runtime traces showing fresh ledger status, balances, trustlines, and network definitions
Assumptions or proofs for node endpoint authenticity and freshness

required_evidence_to_model: XRPL consensus and finality assumptions
Validated-ledger verification traces after submit
A solver model that separates preliminary submit results from final ledger inclusion
unmodeled_components.summary: SubmitResponse fields are preliminary node results; this artifact does not prove transaction finality, consensus inclusion, queue behavior, mempool behavior, or honest peer-server broadcast.

required_evidence_to_model: Build provenance tying source commit to deployed artifacts
Runtime configuration snapshot for ledger endpoints and feature flags
Production trace evidence for representative signing and submit flows

vendor_only_evidence.required_evidence: native vault source or binary audit
negative tests for invalid transaction intents
Lean or Coq proof-consumer kernel report
Provide reviewed production environment profile and source inventory for PORTAL-CXTP-077 and PORTAL-CXTP-078.
Provide real-device runtime trace bundle covering payload intake, review, auth, signing, rejection, expiration, network binding, and broadcast outcomes.
Install or explicitly scope out Apalache, Tamarin, ProVerif, and Coq before claiming those solver lanes.
Integrate the Lean proof-consumer kernel into the production release consumer and clear the proof-consumer-kernel assumption.
